emails.subject: Dental Economics: Is Virtual Dentistry the Future of Solo Practice?
emails.content:
Dental Economics
December 2025 Digital Edition

FEATURE: Is Virtual Dentistry the Future of Solo Practice?

Dr. Rachel Torres launched her teledentistry platform from a 400-sq-ft office in Austin. Two years later, she sees 35 virtual patients a day and has expanded to three states. "The overhead is a fraction of traditional practice," she says. "No front desk, no sterilization costs, no real estate."

But the model isn't without risk. Licensing across state lines remains a patchwork, malpractice insurers are still catching up, and reimbursement rates for virtual-only consults lag behind in-person visits.

For dentists with existing brick-and-mortar practices, the hybrid model may offer the best of both worlds: in-person care for procedures, virtual consults for triage, follow-ups, and second opinions.

Read more at dentaleconomics.com/practice-management/virtual-dentistry-2025

emails.subject: Re: Business plan help - dental startup idea
emails.content:
Hi John,

Thanks for reaching out. Teledentistry is a growing space and we've seen a few healthcare entrepreneurs come through recently. Happy to help.

I've attached two things to get you started:

1. Our standard business plan template (Word doc). It's structured for service businesses. The financial projections tab is the one most people struggle with so don't worry if that takes a few iterations.

2. A one-pager on SBA 7(a) loans. This is the main SBA loan program for startups. For a tech-enabled dental platform you'd probably be looking at $150K-$350K depending on your build-vs-buy approach for the software side. The current rate is prime + 2.75% for loans under $250K.

A couple things to research before our first meeting:
- California telehealth licensing requirements (the Dental Board has specific rules)
- Whether your current malpractice policy covers virtual consults
- Competitor landscape - who else is doing this and how they're structured

I have availability the week of Jan 26 if you want to set up a call. My calendar link is below.

David Huang
Business Advisor, Sacramento SBDC
Sacramento State - College of Business
(916) 555-0734
calendly.com/dhuang-sbdc

emails.subject: Re: Business plan help - dental startup idea
emails.content:
John,

Good question on the SBA loans. Yes, existing debt factors into your debt-to-income ratio. Student loans are common for healthcare professionals so lenders are used to seeing them. The key number is your debt service coverage ratio (DSCR) - they want to see at least 1.25x. We can run the numbers when we talk.

Also, I did some digging on the dental telehealth side since we've had a few inquiries. Here's what I found for California:

- CA Business and Professions Code Section 2290.5 covers telehealth broadly. Dentistry falls under this but the Dental Board has additional requirements.
- You need an existing dentist-patient relationship OR an initial in-person exam before providing telehealth services in most cases. Some exceptions for emergency consultations.
- Informed consent must be obtained and documented before the first virtual visit.
- You must be licensed in CA to treat CA patients virtually. No interstate practice without the other state's license.
- Malpractice: most carriers now offer telehealth riders but you need to ask specifically. Your premium will go up.
- HIPAA applies fully - the platform has to be compliant. Zoom doesn't count. You need a BAA with your video provider.

I attached a summary doc that links to the actual code sections.

Let me know when you want to schedule that call.

David

emails.subject: Your Seeds Are On Their Way! Start Them Right
emails.content:
Hi John,

Your seed order shipped! Here are a few tips to get the best results:

TOMATOES (Better Boy & Roma)
- Start indoors 6-8 weeks before last frost
- Sacramento Zone 9b: last frost around Feb 15, so you're right on time
- Keep soil moist, 70-80F. A sunny windowsill works great.
- Transplant when seedlings are 6-8 inches tall

PEPPERS (California Wonder & Jalapeno)
- Start indoors same timeline as tomatoes
- Peppers like warmth. Bottom heat mats help germination.
- Transplant when nighttime temps stay above 55F

BASIL (Sweet & Genovese)
- Fast growers! Direct sow outdoors after frost danger, or start indoors
- Full sun, well-drained soil
- Pinch flower buds to keep leaves growing

Shop seed starting supplies: burpee.com/seed-starting

Happy growing!
Burpee Seeds & Plants
